  • Mental health and psychosocial support
  • Sport for development
  • Physical activity programming
  • Community-based interventions

Organizations should also have:

  • Robust safeguarding systems
  • Strong organizational governance
  • Experience working in complex operational environments
  • Capacity to deliver large-scale programming
  • A culture of learning and adaptation
  • Willingness to collaborate over the long term

Expression of Interest Focus Areas

The Foundation wants to understand what organizations already bring rather than asking them to create entirely new concepts.

Applicants should be prepared to demonstrate:

  • Existing recovery practice
  • Mental health and psychosocial support expertise
  • Safeguarding policies and systems
  • Operational reach
  • Sport or physical activity experience
  • Organizational learning culture
  • Readiness for long-term collaboration

Key Funding Information

The Recovery Through Sport initiative includes substantial financial support.

Total Funding

€6,000,000

This represents the total funding commitment across all selected strategic partners and future program activities.

Number of Strategic Partners

Between:

  • One
  • Two
  • Three

organizations will ultimately be selected.

Partnership Duration

The initial partnership will last more than three years with opportunities for continued collaboration beyond that period.

Benefits of Becoming an adidas Foundation Strategic Partner

Organizations selected through this Expression of Interest will benefit from:

  • Multi-year funding instead of short project cycles
  • Collaborative program design
  • Long-term strategic partnership
  • Joint development of implementation approaches
  • Access to evidence-informed frameworks
  • Opportunities to contribute to global learning on recovery through sport
  • Close collaboration with the adidas Foundation
  • Participation in shaping an emerging global program area
  • Strong emphasis on safeguarding and quality programming
  • Potential for long-term growth beyond the initial partnership period

Key Dates

Activity Date
Expression of Interest opens 15 July 2026
Submission deadline 30 August 2026 (11:59 PM CEST)

How to Apply

Interested organizations should carefully review the complete Expression of Interest Guidelines before preparing their submission.

The guidelines include:

  • Complete eligibility requirements
  • Partner profile
  • Submission questions
  • Selection criteria
  • Application process
  • Timeline
  • Additional process details

Applications must be submitted before the closing deadline.

APPLY HERE

What Applicants Should Know Before Applying

Applicants should note that:

  • This is a partner selection process.
  • It is not a request for project proposals.
  • Programming will be designed collaboratively.
  • Contexts for implementation will be jointly identified.
  • The Foundation prioritizes evidence, safeguarding, and quality implementation.
  • Long-term collaboration is central to this initiative.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Children and young people living through conflict, disasters, displacement, and humanitarian emergencies often experience profound disruptions to their emotional well-being, education, social relationships, and daily routines.

Recovery Through Sport seeks to explore how structured sport and physical activity can help restore normalcy while complementing broader humanitarian and psychosocial interventions.

By investing in experienced organizations and emphasizing collaborative learning, the adidas Foundation aims to strengthen global evidence on sport-based recovery interventions and develop sustainable approaches that can benefit vulnerable communities worldwide.

Visit HERE for more information about the Adidas Foundation Expression of Interest 2026

Conclusion

The adidas Foundation’s Recovery Through Sport Expression of Interest 2026 represents an important opportunity for organizations working in humanitarian response, child protection, mental health, psychosocial support, and sport for development. Rather than funding isolated projects, the Foundation is investing in long-term strategic partnerships built on trust, evidence, collaboration, and shared learning.

Organizations with proven operational experience, strong safeguarding systems, and a commitment to improving the lives of children and young people affected by crisis are encouraged to review the guidelines and submit their Expression of Interest before the deadline. Successful partners will play a central role in shaping an innovative global program that seeks to harness the power of sport to support recovery, resilience, and hope for young people facing some of the world’s most challenging circumstances.

Application Deadline: 30 August 2026 at 11:59 PM CEST

For more Grant opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post The Adidas Foundation Expression of Interest 2026: Up to €6 Million Available for Strategic Partners in the New Recovery Through Sport Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026: Apply for the Virtual En-ROADS Climate Leadership Training
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
Applications are open for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026, a one-month virtual training programme designed to prepare professionals, educators, policymakers, business leaders and civil society practitioners to facilitate interactive, science-based climate events using the En-ROADS Climate Solutions Simulator. The live cohort will run from 4 August to 4 September 2026, combining the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS […]

The post Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026: Apply for the Virtual En-ROADS Climate Leadership Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are open for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026, a one-month virtual training programme designed to prepare professionals, educators, policymakers, business leaders and civil society practitioners to facilitate interactive, science-based climate events using the En-ROADS Climate Solutions Simulator.

The live cohort will run from 4 August to 4 September 2026, combining the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS online course with twice-weekly Zoom sessions led by members of the Climate Interactive team and other climate-engagement experts.

Participants will learn how to use En-ROADS to explain complex climate and energy systems, explore high-impact climate solutions and lead workshops, simulations and role-playing activities for decision-makers in government, business, finance, education and civil society.

Applications for the August 2026 cohort close on 2 August 2026.

The programme requires an estimated commitment of four to six hours per week. No previous experience with En-ROADS is required, and tuition is donation-based. Participants who cannot afford the suggested contribution may still access the training free of charge.

About the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator is an intensive virtual professional development programme focused on climate education, systems thinking and practical facilitation.

It combines two forms of learning:

  1. Self-paced study through the Mastering En-ROADS course
  2. Live online sessions featuring expert presentations, questions and answers, facilitation guidance and group discussions

The training is intended to help participants move beyond simply understanding climate policy. It prepares them to engage audiences in structured conversations about climate solutions, energy systems, land use, carbon removal, electrification, economic growth and other forces shaping the future of the climate.

Participants will learn how to facilitate events for audiences such as:

  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Corporate executives and business leaders
  • Investors and finance professionals
  • University students and academic institutions
  • Civil society organisations
  • Community leaders
  • Climate networks
  • Professional associations
  • Educational institutions

By the end of the programme, participants should be able to confidently organise and facilitate their own En-ROADS workshops, Climate Action Simulation games and educational activities.

What Is En-ROADS?

En-ROADS is an interactive climate policy simulation developed by Climate Interactive and the MIT Sloan School of Management.

The simulator allows users to explore how different policy choices may affect global temperature outcomes, energy systems, land use and emissions.

Participants can test measures related to areas such as:

  • Renewable energy
  • Coal, oil and natural gas
  • Energy efficiency
  • Electrification
  • Carbon pricing
  • Deforestation
  • Afforestation
  • Methane and other greenhouse gases
  • Population growth
  • Economic growth
  • Technological carbon removal
  • Industry
  • Waste
  • Agricultural emissions
  • Transport and buildings

Instead of presenting climate policy as a fixed list of recommendations, En-ROADS allows participants to experiment with different combinations of solutions and observe how those choices interact.

According to the programme information, En-ROADS has been used by more than 1.5 million people, including over 25,000 leaders from business, government and higher education programmes.

Purpose of the Climate Ambassador Accelerator

Climate change discussions often involve complicated scientific, political and economic questions. Even well-informed audiences may struggle to understand which solutions have the greatest potential impact and how different climate interventions influence one another.

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator is designed to help participants make these discussions more accessible, interactive and actionable.

The programme prepares facilitators to guide audiences through questions such as:

  • Which climate solutions have the greatest potential to reduce future warming?
  • How do energy supply and energy demand interact?
  • What role can electrification play in reducing emissions?
  • How significant are land-use changes and deforestation?
  • What are the limits and opportunities of carbon removal technologies?
  • How do economic growth and population affect emissions?
  • Why are some apparently promising climate solutions less effective than expected?
  • What combination of policies is required to achieve major temperature reductions?

Participants will learn not only how to operate the simulator, but also how to explain its results, manage group discussions and adapt events to different audiences.

Who Should Join the Climate Ambassador Accelerator?

The programme is especially suitable for people who work with decision-makers in:

  • Public policy
  • Government
  • Business
  • Finance
  • Higher education
  • Schools
  • Civil society
  • Sustainable development
  • Climate advocacy
  • Environmental education
  • Corporate sustainability
  • Community engagement

It may also be valuable for professionals who regularly organise workshops, training sessions, public consultations, strategic discussions or educational events.

The training may be a good fit for applicants who:

  • Want to make climate conversations practical and action-oriented
  • Work with audiences that influence policy, investment, education or organisational strategy
  • Want to lead interactive, science-based climate workshops
  • Are interested in climate systems and climate policy
  • Want to add climate facilitation to their professional skills
  • Can commit four to six hours per week for approximately one month
  • Have at least intermediate English proficiency
  • Are prepared to participate in a virtual international learning environment

No previous experience with En-ROADS is required.

Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026 Training Dates

The main programme will run from:

4 August to 4 September 2026

Course modules will become available on 3 August 2026, with access instructions sent to accepted participants by email.

The programme includes five weeks of scheduled learning activities, although the main live training period is described as a one-month programme.

Training Format

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator is delivered entirely online.

The format includes:

  • Short on-demand videos
  • Assigned readings
  • Ungraded quizzes
  • Self-paced online modules
  • Expert-led Zoom presentations
  • Questions and answers with the Climate Interactive team
  • Small-group or breakout discussions
  • Facilitation exercises
  • Recorded sessions for participants who cannot attend live

Participants can work through the online course material according to the weekly schedule.

Live sessions will be held on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 10:00 a.m. EDT, corresponding to GMT-4 during the programme period.

Each live session will last approximately 60 minutes and normally include:

  • Around 30 minutes of core expert content
  • Around 10 minutes of questions and answers
  • Around 20 minutes of group discussion

The live sessions are designed to complement the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS course rather than repeat it.

All sessions will be recorded and made available to participants.

Time Commitment

Participants should be prepared to dedicate approximately:

Four to six hours per week for one month

This includes:

  • Completing the weekly course modules
  • Watching training videos
  • Reviewing readings
  • Taking ungraded quizzes
  • Attending or watching the live sessions
  • Participating in discussions and facilitation exercises
  • Practising with the En-ROADS Simulator

Attending live is recommended, particularly when sessions include group discussions or breakout activities. However, participants who cannot attend every session can use the recordings.

Language Requirement

The training is delivered in English.

Intermediate English proficiency is recommended because participants will need to:

  • Follow expert presentations
  • Read course materials
  • Participate in discussions
  • Ask and answer questions
  • Practise facilitation
  • Communicate with participants from different countries
  • Lead future En-ROADS events

The programme information does not state that an English-language examination certificate is required.

Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026 Cost

The programme uses a donation-based tuition model.

Participants are strongly encouraged to contribute at least US$20, although larger donations are welcomed.

However, applicants who cannot afford the suggested donation may still access the training free of charge.

The En-ROADS Simulator and course materials are made available through support from Climate Interactive’s funders.

The programme should therefore not be described as a paid employment opportunity or a conventional fee-based professional course. It is a donation-supported training opportunity with free access available to those who need it.

Additional Regional Sessions

Participants in selected regions may receive access to additional sessions addressing local climate conditions and regional policy contexts.

The availability, timing and content of these sessions may vary by region.

These sessions may help participants connect global climate modelling with:

  • Regional energy systems
  • National climate commitments
  • Local development priorities
  • Climate-vulnerable communities
  • Regional policy debates
  • Local economic and social conditions

Skills Participants Will Develop

By the end of the programme, participants should be able to:

Explore Climate Solutions

Participants will understand how to use the En-ROADS Simulator to investigate a wide range of climate policies and technological solutions.

Navigate the En-ROADS Simulator

Participants will become familiar with:

  • Core controls
  • Policy sliders
  • Temperature outcomes
  • Energy graphs
  • Emissions data
  • Advanced charts and tools
  • Scenario comparison
  • Model assumptions

Explain System Dynamics

Participants will develop the ability to explain how energy, land use, emissions, population, economic growth and climate systems influence one another.

Facilitate Interactive Workshops

Participants will learn how to structure and lead En-ROADS Climate Workshops for decision-makers and professional audiences.

Lead Climate Action Simulation Games

The training will prepare participants to facilitate role-playing exercises in which audience members represent different sectors or stakeholder groups.

Adapt Events to Different Audiences

Participants will learn how to tailor climate events for:

  • Government leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Corporate boards
  • Business executives
  • Investors
  • Finance professionals
  • University students
  • Educators
  • Civil society organisations

Strengthen Climate Communication

Participants will practise translating complex climate science into understandable, relevant and actionable discussions.

Types of En-ROADS Events

There are three primary ways to use the En-ROADS Simulator with an audience.

1. En-ROADS Climate Workshop

A workshop allows participants to propose climate solutions and immediately observe how those choices affect the projected global temperature increase and other model outcomes.

The facilitator guides the discussion, tests proposals and helps the audience understand why some interventions have greater impact than others.

2. Climate Action Simulation Game

This is a role-playing activity in which participants represent different groups involved in climate decision-making.

Depending on the format, participants may represent governments, businesses, energy companies, civil society organisations, investors or other stakeholders.

The exercise helps participants understand negotiation, competing priorities and the need for coordinated climate action.

3. Student Assignment

Educators may use En-ROADS as part of a classroom assignment, course project or university curriculum.

Students can develop climate scenarios, test assumptions and analyse the interaction between different policies.

En-ROADS Climate Ambassador Certification

Completing the Climate Ambassador Accelerator does not automatically make a participant a certified En-ROADS Climate Ambassador.

To apply for certification, participants must:

  1. Complete the required training
  2. Facilitate at least two En-ROADS events
  3. Submit an application for Ambassador certification

Applications for certification are accepted on a rolling basis, and there is no stated certification deadline.

Participants will continue to have access to the training course after the live programme ends, allowing them to complete remaining materials and prepare their first events.

Benefits of Becoming a Certified En-ROADS Climate Ambassador

Certified Climate Ambassadors become part of a global community of climate facilitators.

Ambassadors may have opportunities to:

  • Participate in quarterly online gatherings
  • Attend special events
  • Collaborate with climate leaders from different regions
  • Receive support from the Climate Interactive team
  • Access selected professional opportunities
  • Expand their facilitation portfolio
  • Be listed in the Climate Ambassador directory
  • Make it easier for organisations to contact them
  • Use En-ROADS to inform policy and organisational strategy
  • Build professional credibility in climate education and engagement

Most Ambassadors are expected to identify and organise their own opportunities to facilitate events within their professional and community networks.

Climate Interactive may occasionally share facilitation opportunities, but certification does not guarantee employment, paid assignments or direct placement in events.

What Happens After the Live Training?

After the scheduled Zoom sessions conclude, participants will retain access to the online course.

There is no stated deadline for completing the remaining self-paced content.

Participants may use the post-training period to:

  • Review recorded sessions
  • Practise using the simulator
  • Complete unfinished modules
  • Design an En-ROADS workshop
  • Identify suitable audiences
  • Facilitate their first two events
  • Apply for Climate Ambassador certification

This flexible access is particularly useful for participants who cannot complete every activity during August.

Can Applicants With an Existing Mastering En-ROADS Account Apply?

Yes.

People who already have a Mastering En-ROADS account can apply to the Climate Ambassador Accelerator.

Accepted participants can sign in with their existing account after receiving programme instructions and register for the live sessions.

Having an existing account does not prevent participation.

What If a Participant Cannot Attend Every Live Session?

Live attendance is encouraged but not mandatory for every session.

All sessions will be recorded and made available to participants.

Participants should attend live where possible because group discussions and breakout activities may be difficult to reproduce through recordings. Nevertheless, the recorded format provides flexibility for applicants in different time zones or with professional commitments.

What If Someone Cannot Participate in August 2026?

Applicants who cannot join the August cohort may still take the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS course throughout the year.

The course is available year-round through Climate Interactive’s online learning platform.

However, participants taking only the self-paced course may not receive the same live expert sessions, cohort discussions and networking experience offered through the Climate Ambassador Accelerator.

Climate Ambassador Accelerator Information Session

An information session was scheduled for 7 July 2026 at 8:00 a.m. EDT.

The session was intended to help prospective applicants understand:

  • The training format
  • The time commitment
  • The En-ROADS Simulator
  • Certification requirements
  • Live-session expectations
  • Whether the programme matched their professional goals

Because the scheduled date has passed, prospective applicants should check whether a recording is available.

How to Apply for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026

Interested applicants must complete the official programme application to reserve a place in the August cohort.

Before applying, candidates should be prepared to provide information about:

  • Their professional background
  • Their involvement in climate, policy, business, finance, education or civil society
  • The audiences they work with
  • Their interest in En-ROADS
  • How they expect to use the training
  • Their ability to commit four to six hours per week
  • Their English-language ability
  • Their plans for facilitating future climate events

Applicants should submit the application before the deadline and monitor their email for acceptance instructions.

Programme modules will become available on 3 August 2026, making the period between the application deadline and the programme launch very short.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

Applications for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator close on:

2 August 2026

The training begins on 4 August 2026, while course modules and participant instructions are expected to become available on 3 August 2026.

Applicants should apply as early as possible rather than waiting until the final day.

Important Dates

  • Information session: 7 July 2026 at 8:00 a.m. EDT
  • Application deadline: 2 August 2026
  • Modules available: 3 August 2026
  • First live session: 4 August 2026
  • Main training period: 4 August–4 September 2026
  • Final scheduled week: 31 August–5 September 2026
  • Climate Ambassador certification: Rolling applications with no deadline

Important Points to Consider Before Applying

Prospective participants should understand that:

  • The programme is conducted in English.
  • Intermediate English proficiency is recommended.
  • The programme requires four to six hours per week.
  • Live sessions are scheduled according to Eastern Daylight Time.
  • The course is virtual.
  • Live attendance is recommended but recordings are available.
  • No prior En-ROADS experience is required.
  • Tuition is donation-based.
  • A contribution of at least US$20 is encouraged.
  • Participants who cannot afford the contribution may access the training free of charge.
  • Completing the course does not automatically result in Ambassador certification.
  • Certification requires facilitating at least two En-ROADS events.
  • Certification does not guarantee employment or paid facilitation opportunities.

Conclusion

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026 offers an accessible and practical route for professionals who want to lead stronger, more informed climate conversations.

By combining the Mastering En-ROADS online course with live expert-led sessions, the programme helps participants understand climate-system dynamics while developing the practical skills required to facilitate workshops, role-playing games and educational activities.

The programme is particularly relevant for professionals working with decision-makers in government, business, finance, education and civil society. It may also be valuable for climate advocates, sustainability professionals, educators and community leaders who want a structured, science-based way to engage their networks.

No previous experience with En-ROADS is required, and participants who cannot afford the suggested donation may still take part for free. However, applicants must be ready to commit four to six hours per week and participate in an English-language virtual learning environment.

Applications close on 2 August 2026, and the programme begins on 4 August 2026.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global job opportunities, click here.

The post Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026: Apply for the Virtual En-ROADS Climate Leadership Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026–2027 United Nations UNSCAR Application Cycle: Apply for Funding to Advance Conventional Arms Regulation and Disarmament Initiatives
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
The United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA) has officially launched the 2026–2027 United Nations Trust Facility Supporting Cooperation on Arms Regulation (UNSCAR) Application Cycle, inviting eligible organizations worldwide to submit project proposals that strengthen conventional arms regulation, improve weapons management, and contribute to international peace and security. UNSCAR is a multi-donor trust fund established […]

The post 2026–2027 United Nations UNSCAR Application Cycle: Apply for Funding to Advance Conventional Arms Regulation and Disarmament Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA) has officially launched the 2026–2027 United Nations Trust Facility Supporting Cooperation on Arms Regulation (UNSCAR) Application Cycle, inviting eligible organizations worldwide to submit project proposals that strengthen conventional arms regulation, improve weapons management, and contribute to international peace and security.

UNSCAR is a multi-donor trust fund established to provide focused and effective financial support for initiatives that enhance the implementation of international conventional arms control instruments. Through competitive annual funding calls, the programme supports innovative projects that build national and regional capacities, strengthen policy frameworks, and reduce the risks associated with illicit conventional weapons.

Organizations working in disarmament, arms control, security sector reform, weapons management, or related research are encouraged to submit proposals before the application deadline.

About the United Nations UNSCAR Programme

The United Nations Trust Facility Supporting Cooperation on Arms Regulation (UNSCAR) is managed by the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA). The trust fund was created to strengthen global cooperation on conventional arms regulation by providing financial assistance to organizations implementing projects that improve arms control, enhance weapons and ammunition management, and support international peace and security.

Each year, UNSCAR issues a Call for Proposals through a competitive selection process. The programme funds projects that align with its strategic objectives and address priority themes identified in the annual call.

In addition to the regular funding cycle, UNSCAR also provides Special-Circumstances Projects to respond rapidly to emergency situations involving weapons management.

Objectives of the Programme

UNSCAR seeks to:

  • Strengthen conventional arms regulation.
  • Improve national and regional weapons management systems.
  • Support the implementation of international arms control agreements.
  • Build institutional and technical capacities.
  • Promote international cooperation on disarmament.
  • Reduce illicit trafficking and misuse of conventional weapons.
  • Contribute to sustainable peace, security, and development.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are welcomed from organizations with legal status, including:

  • United Nations system partners
  • Regional organizations
  • International organizations
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Research institutes

Member States that wish to access UNSCAR funding should partner with an eligible organization that can submit a proposal on their behalf.

Project Requirements

To be considered for funding, project proposals must:

  • Be consistent with the objectives of UNSCAR.
  • Align with the annual selection criteria, including funding limits and implementation timelines.
  • Address one or more priority areas identified in the annual Call for Proposals.

UNSCAR particularly encourages the submission of small-scale, high-impact projects.

Special-Circumstances Projects

In addition to the annual funding cycle, UNSCAR accepts applications throughout the year for Special-Circumstances Projects.

These projects:

  • Receive funding of up to USD 200,000.
  • Support rapid responses to emergency situations involving weapons management.
  • Can be submitted at any time during the year.

Application Process

Applicants must use the official UNSCAR application templates available on the programme website.

Each application must include:

  1. Completed Application Form.
  2. Logical Framework (Logframe), including a detailed implementation plan.
  3. Detailed Project Budget.

All documents must:

  • Be completed in English.
  • Be submitted together.
  • Be sent before the application deadline.

Once submitted, proposals and budgets cannot be amended.

Selection Process

Following the submission deadline, all applications undergo a competitive review process.

Projects are evaluated based on:

  • Relevance to UNSCAR objectives.
  • Technical quality of the proposal.
  • Expected impact.
  • Feasibility of implementation.
  • Budget justification.
  • Contribution to international cooperation on conventional arms regulation.

Successful projects are expected to begin implementation from 1 January 2027.

Requirements for Selected Applicants

Organizations selected for funding will work closely with UNODA to finalize project documentation before signing the Grant Agreement.

Additional documents may include:

  • Detailed implementation schedule.
  • Risk assessment and mitigation strategy.
  • Evidence of support from recipient governments (where applicable).
  • Coordination plans with other UNSCAR implementing partners.

UNSCAR encourages close collaboration among implementing organizations to maximize impact and avoid duplication of activities.

Monitoring and Reporting

Successful applicants are required to maintain high standards of programme management and accountability.

Implementing partners must submit:

  • Interim narrative reports.
  • Interim financial reports.
  • Final narrative reports.
  • Final financial reports.

UNSCAR also encourages applicants to include robust monitoring, evaluation, and auditing mechanisms within their project proposals.

Important Dates

  • Application Cycle Opens: July 2026
  • Online Briefing Session: 28 July 2026 (9:00 AM EDT)
  • Application Deadline: 31 August 2026 at 5:00 PM EDT
  • Project Implementation Begins: 1 January 2027

Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should download the official application forms and guidelines from the UNSCAR website before preparing their proposals.

Application Link: https://www.un.org/disarmament/unscar/

Applications and supporting documents should be submitted electronically to:

Email: unscar-unoda@un.org

For detailed guidance, eligibility requirements, and downloadable application forms, visit the official UNSCAR webpage.

Conclusion

The 2026–2027 UNSCAR Application Cycle offers a valuable opportunity for eligible organizations to secure funding for projects that strengthen conventional arms regulation, improve weapons management, and promote international peace and security. Through its competitive funding process, UNSCAR supports innovative initiatives that build capacity, encourage international cooperation, and contribute to safer communities worldwide. Organizations with impactful project ideas are encouraged to prepare strong proposals and submit their applications before 31 August 2026.

Discover more grants opportunities like this on the OFY Opportunities webpage. 

The post 2026–2027 United Nations UNSCAR Application Cycle: Apply for Funding to Advance Conventional Arms Regulation and Disarmament Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
